title: Longest Increasing Order Streak
description: Find all customers whose longest strictly increasing order-amount streak is equal to the global maximum streak length.
sql_solution: WITH t AS (SELECT *, CASE WHEN amount > LAG(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END inc FROM orders), grp AS (SELECT *, SUM(CASE WHEN inc = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) g FROM t) SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM grp GROUP BY customer_id, g HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(cnt) FROM (SELECT customer_id, g, COUNT(*) cnt FROM grp GROUP BY customer_id, g));
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

sequence_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))
group_window = (
    Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
    .orderBy(to_date(col("date")))
    .rowsBetween(Window.unboundedPreceding, Window.currentRow)
)

increasing_flags = (
    orders.withColumn("prev_amount", lag("amount").over(sequence_window))
    .withColumn(
        "inc",
        when(col("amount") > col("prev_amount"), lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0))
    )
)

streak_groups = increasing_flags.withColumn(
    "g",
    sum(when(col("inc") == 0, lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0))).over(group_window)
)

streak_counts = streak_groups.groupBy("customer_id", "g").agg(count(lit(1)).alias("cnt"))
max_streak = streak_counts.agg(max("cnt").alias("max_cnt"))

result = (
    streak_counts.crossJoin(max_streak)
    .filter(col("cnt") == col("max_cnt"))
    .select("customer_id")
    .distinct()
)
